title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Language is a (0) DYNAMIC system that is constantly evolving to reflect social changes. The (17) of new technologies often leads to the (18) of original words and phrases. While some (19) worry that digital communication is (20) our grammar, others argue it is simply a new form of (21). The (22) of the internet has also encouraged the (23) of loanwords from different cultures. This (24) makes language more versatile and inclusive. (25), languages have always borrowed from one another, and this process is unlikely to stop. As we continue to connect with people from around the globe, our ways of speaking and writing will keep adapting, ensuring that communication remains a vibrant and essential tool for human interaction and the shared (26) of our collective experience in an ever-changing world.

example.explanation: Adjective required to describe the 'system'; DYNAMO → DYNAMIC.

questions.explanation: Noun required after the definite article; APPEAR → APPEARANCE.
Plural noun for people who study language required; LANGUAGE → LINGUISTS.
Present participle for continuous tense; WEAK → WEAKENING.
Noun required as the subject; GLOBAL → GLOBALISATION.
Noun required after the definite article; ADOPT → ADOPTION.
